title: pterocarpos peltarius
intro: Pterocarpos peltarius, also known as the Pterocarpus marsupium, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, classified as a deciduous tree belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly due to the presence of flavonoids and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Pterocarpos peltarius include its potential to manage diabetes, alleviate cardiovascular issues, and act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of extracts or decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or added to traditional remedies.
